title: Share Board w/ Link - Can't view Subitems?
excerpt:
Hi there, 
I have a board I want to share with an outside consultant. I don’t want to invite them as a Guest, but just share the board with a link. But it appears that when I share with the link that none of the sub-item…

posts.author: rebekahpearson (Rebekah Pearson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

I have a board I want to share with an outside consultant. I don’t want to invite them as a Guest, but just share the board with a link. But it appears that when I share with the link that none of the sub-items show up.

How can I share a board and make sure someone can see all the sub-items?

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @rebekahpearson, good question!

There’s no way to share a board via link and have the subitems show up at the moment, as subitems are still in beta.

Does that help clarify? The other alternative for now would be to add the outside consultant as a guest to the board (which sounds like it might not be ideal for you) or screenshot the board and subitems and email it to the outside consultant.

posts.author: rebekahpearson (Rebekah Pearson)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks… we figured it must not be available at this time so we’ve found another solution to work around.

posts.author: celestew (Celeste Wickard)
posts.cookedHtml: do we have any update on this? I don’t see it on the subitems roadmap

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Same question as other users, I design some boards using subitems and I have to share it with external teams and it’s not possible for us to redesign all tables, could you please let us know when it will be possible to see subitems on shared boards please?

Many thanks for your feedback and have a nice day.

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Anyone from Monday can let us know if it will be possible and when exactly?

Thanks for your feedback.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Julien78, welcome to the community and thanks so much for following up on this! It’s not on our roadmap at the moment, but I will certainly pass on your feedback to our team. I can see how useful such a feature would be when sharing items with external teams.

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @TRB-monday.com Many thanks for your feedback! It will be very useful for us many thanks for your help.

Have a nice day!

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Julien78 of course! I can understand it would be really helpful, and hope you are having a nice holiday season!

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Talia @TRB-monday.com, wish you the best for this new year, did you get any update about sub items visibilty?

Have a nice day.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Julien78, hope you are doing well in the new year too! Apologies for the delay in response here.

I just checked with my team—we still don’t have an ETA for it, unfortunately (meaning it won’t come out this quarter), but I’m going to advocate for internally and hope it’s something we can do later in the year.

posts.author: jescobar (Jonathan Escobar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Talia, @TRB-monday.com , I’m in a similar boat as @Julien78. My organization is able to show almost everything we need to the public, except for the subitems column, which we use heavily for resource management. Please feel free to let your internal team know that another person out in the world is looking for this feature to be visible as well.

Thank you!

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @TRB-monday.com Any updates about our request please?
Thanks for your feedback.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Julien78 and @jescobar, still no updates on this (the developers responsible for this area will not give an ETA), but I have passed your feedback to them, and will make sure they know how impactful it would be for you! I will keep you updated if anything changes.

posts.author: MichaelCalvinJones (Michael Jones)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com Just bumping this as it seems this can be a pretty useful update. Our agency would greatly benefit from this and it seems rather straightforward. Appreciate your assistance pushing this internally.

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everybody,

Sorry but it’s really urgent for us to be able to share ALL items & subitems with other people.

As a reminder, we are asking about this feature since August 2020.

Please let us know when it will be available it’s urgent for us.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: Same for us… Its really important for our workflows

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MichaelCalvinJones, @Julien78, and @hlopezvc! I’m continuing to try to push this internally—I understand it’s important for your workflows and will keep you updated when I get news.

For anyone else reading this—feel free to share how using subitems in broadcast/this shared view would fit into your use case! The more feedback we get the more likely it is to happen.

posts.author: celestew (Celeste Wickard)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TRB-monday.com

For us, I am the implementation project manager for our company and I need to be able to share a list of the project tasks with timeline. Each task has sub items and that’s the most important part as it lists specific tasks that are important to be completed. I don’t want to add users as guest, but I want to be able to share a non-editable view of the items, but since sub items do not show up, they cannot see the tasks necessary to update.

posts.author: Con-Rad (Con Rad)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this feature!

posts.author: gwheat (Garrett)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @TRB-monday.com, wanted to reach out and let you know this is a need we have as well. Within our organization, we would like to share a board with tens/hundreds of items, all with subitems (links as resources).

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @TRB-monday.com Any update about this case?

Please could definitely give us a feature availability deadline? It’s very important for us and we are asking about that since enough months…

Thanks for pushing this feature on your Roadmap.

Have a nice day.

posts.author: vhurrell (Victoria Hurrell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @TRB-monday.com - we would also REALLY benefit from viewable Subitems in Shared views. Please push this forward if you can.

Thank you!!

posts.author: gdenure (Graham DeNure)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d also like to chime in here and just say that this feature would be extremely useful for our business, as we also have a similar use case as everyone else posting here. I do see it on the subitems roadmap, which is great, but with the release date being TBD, which is not so great.
Can anyone suggest a workaround? Also, is there any way to share a public link view to a board and include the ability for the viewer to read updates and info boxes?

posts.author: celestew (Celeste Wickard)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com - this needs more attention than I feel it is receiving. Unfortunately, I have had to go in and redo all my boards (10+) to where there is no subitems or subitems are things that we would already hide from customer facing views. Needless to say, it was cumbersome to do this.

posts.author: jenstratton (Jen Stratton)
posts.cookedHtml: Appreciate you pushing it, this would be very helpful for what we plan to use Monday for as well!

posts.author: maxim (Maxim Krušina)
posts.cookedHtml: Need this! Please, hurry!

posts.author: KateTerzieva (Kate Terzieva)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to add my vote to this topic - sub items are really handy, but if they are not seen on share links then that makes them pretty useless.

posts.author: AntonvanChristus (Anton van Christhus)
posts.cookedHtml: We also realy need this feature!

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @TRB-monday.com please could you help us with this feature and let us know?
For now I have to export Monday boards Excel = That’s exactly what I don’t like to do, Excel isn’t dynamic… We are rolling back to this horrible tool just about this issue…

Thanks for pushing all our requests.

Have a nice day.

posts.author: jescobar (Jonathan Escobar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @TRB-monday.com,

I’m with everyone else and would like to see some traction on this feature. After reviewing the subitems roadmap, I noticed an item that references “Board Views,” and it’s release is “TBD;” does this encompass what we’re looking for in this thread?

Thanks!

posts.author: kdallons (Kat Dallons)
posts.cookedHtml: Also want to chime in as this is a very important feature we need. For client-facing tracking, we end up having to completely redo the same tracking (minus some internal info) in google sheets…we’re doing double the work where we were hoping that Monday would help with consolidating tasks.

posts.author: Sprints (Glenn )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @TRB-monday.com at the agency I work we would also really like this feature to be added as soon as possible!

posts.author: johnnaccarato (John)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a game changer. We have brand deals with hundreds of deliverables listed as subitems and want them to be able to see their status.

posts.author: johnnaccarato (John)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be huge to share subitems to other companies like our that has hundreds of brand deals with deliverables as subitems.

posts.author: johnnaccarato (John)
posts.cookedHtml:
In our company, we have brand deals with several other companies that have deliverables. We have video creation and where they are in the process, the links to scripts, release dates, we have email campaigns, along with interviewing on podcasts, etc. The brand deals are 1-2 years long with hundreds of deliverables and tied to connected boards.

Only thing the brand can see is the first line, and none of the important details.

I would hope this wouldn’t be a major problem since the linking is already working with the general line, just code it to include all of it.

Please.

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any updates about this case?

Thanks…

posts.author: ChrisCilino (Chris Cilino)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m also very interested in this feature.

posts.author: Julien78 (Julien BA)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

@TRB-monday.com Could you please let us know when the feature will available? 1 year waiting for that…

Thanks.

posts.author: rbichener (Ross Bichener)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’ve got a projects board with hundreds of item level projects (200+ different clients), the sub-items are the day to day operations for each project. We want our clients to be able to see these day to day operations for their project, as they change on a daily basis. So it’d be good for them to be able to see everything related to that project in one place instead of having to constantly phone/email us to check on the status of those sub-item operations.
We’d then create a filtered view so that only projects relevant to that client are shown and share that view with them. But currently they can’t see sub-items on a shared board.

posts.cookedHtml: Hi @TRB-monday.com just adding my name to the list here that this would be very (very very very) important to have this feature implemented. Thanks!

posts.cookedHtml: We would also love to have this option - our project board has each pulse represent a project for a specific partner/client/requestor and the subitems represent our timeline. Since our board contains all our projects, we don’t want to share full access to the board, just their projects. Will this be coming soon? We’d love to share the subitems on (filtered) public share links.

posts.cookedHtml: Ditto everything above. This is really important for us as our items contain projects and the subitems contain the key milestone dates. It is hard to share externally without being able to include the key milestone dates for everyone.

THANKS.

posts.cookedHtml: Please add this feature. Our organisation is new to Monday.com, but building out the subitems capability (nested tasks/hierarchy) is essential in order to compete with other products such as Smartsheet. I need to share our subitem schedule with the vendors we are working with.

posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Julien78, @jarodthornton, @tom_certino, @jane.sullivan, @nolanhausler, @epmorris, @MarkMcG, @cambeaudoin, @rbichener, @ChrisCilino, and everyone else, great news!!

Please DM me on the community (here) if you are interested and let me know your monday.com URL in the format “xyz.monday.com”. I’ll have the developers grant it to you, and we’d be eager to get your feedback!

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @nicole_elphic, @epmorris, @EPerez, @Kevin_deRijk, @hlopezvc, and everyone who sent me DMs, I’ve put in a request to the developers for this feature to be granted to your accounts! Thanks so much for your interest in broadcasting subitems and for being willing to test this new feature

We would be really grateful if you are able to respond with feedback on the feature. Thanks @comunicacaofundiagua for sending feedback already!

I received DMs from some people but haven’t been able to receive them from others, so feel free to keep listing your monday.com URLs directly in this thread if you’d like me to request this feature for your account.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Connexall, @epmorris, @Kelderic, @ConsultantCoach, @danny.lugo, @mondaylearner it should be available now! We released it to 100% of non-Enterprise accounts 2 days ago. If it’s not working (or if you’re on Enterprise), please write to support@monday.com so we can help you there Otherwise, we’d be happy to gather any feedback here!

posts.author: Kelderic (Andy)
posts.cookedHtml: Can confirm, it’s working well. Thanks! Now if we just had ability to deeplink to specific items in the public view, everything would be perfect.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Great! Passed on your feedback @Kelderic and this feature is now released to all Enterprise accounts as well

posts.author: celestew (Celeste Wickard)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com did something change with the feature that is preventing subitems from being able to be hidden from a shared view?

posts.author: hchoffmanv (Clay Hoffman)
posts.cookedHtml: @TRB-monday.com apologies if I missed this somewhere but is Monday looking to make updates viewable when you share a link?

posts.author: Kevin_deRijk (Kevin de Rijk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@TRB-monday.com
Hi, currently we’re using this feature and it is working well! People with the link are able to view the subitems and information such as dates and the status of the subitem.
After some testing the feature is only working with the “table” view, which we have set as board default. Is it possible to use the feature in a shared calendar view as well? We would love to share the calendar view with subitems since we use it as a content planning in our communications department. If that’s possible, people who deliver content are able to track their posts easily and see its scheduled day/time.

posts.author: kdallons (Kat Dallons)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi There seems to be no way to edit what is seen in the linked board. For example, I’d like to share a filtered version of my board with clients so they only see pertinent info to them. Can you please let me know if this feature is being added?

posts.author: nolanhausler (Nolan Hausler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Looks like our account has this feature now (yay!) but when you expand subitems in a link view, I do not see any additional subitem columns (ie. date, status, etc. of the subitems). Anyone know what might be going on?

@TRB-monday.com

posts.author: ivangarcia (Ivan Garcia _Appfire_)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there!

If you want to share dashboards externally – and you’re up for trying an app – you definitely have to give Dashboard Hub a try. It is developed by Appfire, company I work for.

Dashboard Hub is an app that empowers anyone in the team to easily create and share powerful dashboards with formulas, custom charts and nearly 100 metrics – regardless of technical skills.

It allows to create secure and unique public links to share dashboards and reports to external stakeholders in seconds. See how it works in the GIF below

Why don’t you try it out 14 days for free?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey everyone! For those that might not be aware this feature has now been released to all accounts. If you’re unable to view subitems (and their associated data) in shared views, please reach out to support@monday.com so our technical specialists can investigate

posts.author: WeymannServices (David Singer)
posts.cookedHtml: STILL WAITING - I’ve reviewed the posts here - and for years it seems - people have been asking Monday about this feature/capacity - and - here we are - another year later - and I’m chatting with customer support now to see if this is resolved.

posts.author: cgrzybowski (Corey Grzybowski)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature became available but what if we do not want to share and display the subitems. At least give the option to hide or show.
